If you can, please chip in $3 or whatever makes sense to you right now to help hit our April fundraising goal before this huge deadline.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fe13/45c30650/8fbc5708/6697ac31/3487377406/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWFwcjE5ZW9tIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDQyOGVtMi1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-apr19eom?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190428em2-full")**

name: Danielle Hirshberg, Team Hickenlooper
subject: While my phone charges
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
body:
We're powering up to get John on the debate stage this June. | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771691/691c9bd/6c423800/11a4a692/1303775041/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_template")
---
| Team, I'm Danielle, the campaign's Digital Creative Strategist. I'm usually on the go, but today I'm taking a beat to write this email while my phone charges up--a pretty common thing since I'm constantly using it to take photos, videos, and notes that chronicle this campaign and the transformative conversations John is having every day on the trail.

**Watching him in action every day, I can tell you that John is a library of stories about the people he meets.** Whether you're a student worried about tuition bills, a senior worried about prescription drug costs, or a working parent reflecting on how you're going to get ahead, John will listen. He will empathize. He will remember what you said. He will talk it out with us in the car immediately after, and refer back to your stories long after we leave.

John is a storyteller. He is committed to finding every opportunity he can to tell your stories on the national stage--[**_and we 're especially focused on gaining the momentum and donor base we need to bring them to the debate stage in June._**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771691/691c9bd/6c423800/11a4a68e/1303775041/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_fr_20190428_DH_20190428-fr-DH-chargedbump-ndnr_ndnr_l1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Qualifying for the debates requires a combination of grassroots fundraising and donor count, and we need to grow both to make sure that John takes the stage at the first debate in Miami to offer a voice that our workers, our students, our seniors, and our immigrants need so deeply in this time of division.

[**_Can you step up to make your first donation now and keep John on track to advocate for everyday Americans at the debates?_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771691/691c9bd/6c423800/11a4a68f/1303775041/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_fr_20190428_DH_20190428-fr-DH-chargedbump-ndnr_ndnr_l2&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Charged up, ready to go,
Danielle

Danielle Hirshberg
Digital Creative Strategist
Hickenlooper 2020 [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771691/691c9bd/6c423800/11a4a68d/1303775041/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_fr_20190428_DH_20190428-fr-DH-chargedbump-ndnr_ndnr_contribbutton&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

| | Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
---

Keep us company on the campaign trail by joining us on [_**Facebook**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771691/691c9bd/6c423800/11a4a689/1303775041/VEsA/ "https://www.facebook.com/JohnHickenlooper/"), [_**Twitter**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771691/691c9bd/6c423800/11a4a686/1303775041/VEsB/ "https://twitter.com/hickenlooper"), [_**Instagram**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771691/691c9bd/6c423800/11a4a684/1303775041/VEsO/ "https://www.instagram.com/johnhickenlooper/"), and [_**YouTube**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771691/691c9bd/6c423800/11a4a685/1303775041/VEsP/ "https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC82O0Byp_ny13O0LkO1UpUg/featured").

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: The existential threat we face
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
Today, we are unveiling our framework to fight climate change. The action steps enclosed begin on day one when we are elected to serve in the White House. But given the gravity of the work that lies ahead, we know that this fight will require much more than a president signing a handful of executive orders. We will need to fully mobilize our democracy and economy — every single one of us, joining together — to defeat the gravest of threats to humanity.

| [en Español](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17469?t=5&akid=29069%2E444211%2EDCZ41m)
---|---
#
The greatest threat we face — which will test our country, our democracy, every single one of us — is climate change.
The planet has warmed more than 1-degree Celsius just since 1980 — helping to cause historic fires, floods, hurricanes and droughts seen throughout the world and in the U.S. This warming is caused by our own excesses, our emissions and our own inaction in the face of the facts and the science.
But we have 12 years to shift course and avert the deadliest impacts that will follow warming of 3 or 4 degrees, or even more. The next president must ensure that we unleash the ingenuity and political will of hundreds of millions of Americans to meet this moment before it's too late.
That’s why today I want to share our plan to fight climate change. The action steps enclosed begin on day one. But given the gravity of the work that lies ahead, we know that this fight will require much more than a president signing a handful of executive orders. We will need a full mobilization of our democracy and economy — every single one of us, joining together — to defeat the gravest of threats to humanity.
[Take a moment now to read more about our plan on our campaign website. Then forward this email to your friends and family — we need everyone all-in on this.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17468?source=ema20190429-29069&t=1&akid=29069%2E444211%2EDCZ41m)
We begin by cutting pollution on day one, reentering the Paris Climate Agreement and curbing fossil fuel production on public lands. We continue by making a historic investment in infrastructure, innovation, and people that mobilizes $5 trillion over the next 10 years to confront climate change head on. And we will leave nothing to chance: our administration will establish legally enforceable standards to guarantee that we achieve net-zero emissions by the year 2050 and get halfway there by 2030.
In all of this, we will be there for and lift up the people and communities most affected by the dangers and destruction of global warming. Our administration will increase funding ten-fold to help vulnerable communities prepare for extreme weather. And we will be there for these communities when disasters do strike.
Much as The Greatest Generation met the existential threat to the western democracies nearly 80 years ago, and at the same time helped to lift millions of Americans into the middle class, we have an opportunity now to meet an existential threat to this planet by ensuring that public health policy, economic policy, and the engine of a far more conscientious capitalism is put to work in service of our ability to meet this challenge.
[There’s much more in the plan to read, so I would encourage you to take a look at our website. But I also want you to know that this is just the beginning. As we travel across the country and hear more great ideas from the American people in their communities, we will be adding more policy proposals along the way.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17468?source=ema20190429-29069&t=2&akid=29069%2E444211%2EDCZ41m)

This moment of maximum peril may also be our moment of maximum potential. Let us show ourselves, our children, and the world that when we come together, there is no limit to what we can achieve.
Beto

| My dear supporters,

Ever since our launch on April 8th, I’ve been emphatic about my one goal: to get on the Democratic debate stage and alert Americans to the immorality of our military industrial complex.

The response to our mission has been so incredibly heartening. We’ve felt your support - not just online, not just in the number of donors, but also in polling.

And yet, I have heard chatter that our efforts do not amount to a “serious campaign” because we intend to drop out after the debates. I am confused. In many ways, I find our campaign to be, perhaps, the only serious campaign that’s running.

We aspire for a higher goal than the office of the President of the United States of America. While other candidates run on personality and egotism, our campaign is defining the change and progress we need to live dignified lives -- including the communities abroad that we have devastated with our government’s greed and paranoia.

It is odd that our campaign for peace is not perceived as “serious.” Even stranger considering I am a former United States Senator.

So here it is: I am running to win. (Just as much as Seth Moulton, John Delaney, John Hickenlooper, Tim Ryan, or Eric Swalwell are.)

I don’t intend to be a disruption, I intend to hold up a mirror. America’s elections come and go, but the blood on our hands gets thicker by the day.

Accountability is long overdue. Let’s start here: spread the word and donate to Mike Gravel to get us on that damn stage.

subject: Bask in the Booming Economy
body:
"The Trump economy is going full speed ahead and hitting milestones few predicted could be achieved." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a33b192d55&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Bask in the Booming Economy**

“The Trump economy is going full speed ahead and hitting milestones few predicted could be achieved. In the first three months of this year, the U.S. economy grew at 3.2 percent annual rate, exceeding expectations,” the _Boston Herald_ editorial board writes. “The economic good news has many experts retracting their warnings of recession they had in store for 2019.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=db1d450147&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The New York Times deleted a political cartoon over the weekend after acknowledging that it contained ‘anti-Semitic tropes,’ an episode decried as the latest example of rising anti-Semitism on the left,” _[The Washington Times](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=67eea7f5d6&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “The New York Times editor’s note was described by some as an apology, although the message did not actually apologize for running the cartoon.” In a statement, the American Jewish Committee said it would not accept the non-apology. “How many @nytimes editors looked at a cartoon that would not have looked out of place on a white supremacist website and thought it met the paper’s editorial standards?”
---
|
---
| “Democrats are experts at deception, but even they have their work cut out for them if they want to convince Pennsylvanians that President Trump’s tax cuts have been anything but a major win for the middle class,” David Urban writes in the _[Philadelphia Inquirer](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e238f61ecd&e=3ee4985eea)_. “According to the Heritage Foundation, the average tax cut for individuals in Pennsylvania in 2018 was $1,169, representing a decrease in payment of nearly 12 percent from 2017. The [Tax Cuts and Jobs Act] didn’t just give Pennsylvanians a hefty tax break, though. The GOP tax law also led to better wages, improved employment prospects, and lower utility prices for families across the state.”
---
|
---
| “The border crisis has been manufactured alright – by Congress, which has failed in its duty, by the activists who encourage illegal border crossing; by the judiciary, whose activist judges prevent the executive branch from protecting the country; and by the leftist media that defends a porous border,” write Reps. Andy Biggs (R-AZ), Sean Duffy (R-WI), Matt Gaetz (R-FL), Duncan Hunter (R-CA), and John Joyce (R-PA) in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=313d58c1b5&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “President Trump’s trade czar, Peter Navarro, said Thursday night during a speech at the Institute of Politics’ JFK Jr. Forum that the administration’s efforts to remake American trade policies, pressure China to reform its practices, and revamp the tariff system are boosting the American economy,” Elizabeth Gehrman reports for _[The Harvard Gazette](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6f168a416e&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The president’s tariffs on steel and aluminum represent one of the most successful applications of a defensive trade polity in U.S. history . . . Billions of dollars of new investment have poured into these industries.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e74ae7b59e&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=642fa6b5de&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=0f2354f786)
---

clean_body: A message about our campaign from Senator Gravel | | | | | | Hey, so we accidentally used an old subject line for the last email we sent (even though we still believe it), but here is the email again because we still think everyone should see it. \-- My dear supporters, Ever since our launch on April 8th, I’ve been emphatic about my one goal: to get on the Democratic debate stage and alert Americans to the immorality of our military industrial complex. The response to our mission has been so incredibly heartening. We’ve felt your support - not just online, not just in the number of donors, but also in polling. And yet, I have heard chatter that our efforts do not amount to a “serious campaign” because we intend to drop out after the debates. I am confused. In many ways, I find our campaign to be, perhaps, the only serious campaign that’s running. We aspire for a higher goal than the office of the President of the United States of America. While other candidates run on personality and egotism, our campaign is defining the change and progress we need to live dignified lives -- including the communities abroad that we have devastated with our government’s greed and paranoia. It is odd that our campaign for peace is not perceived as “serious.” Even stranger considering I am a former United States Senator. So here it is: I am running to win. (Just as much as Seth Moulton, John Delaney, John Hickenlooper, Tim Ryan, or Eric Swalwell are.) I don’t intend to be a disruption, I intend to hold up a mirror. America’s elections come and go, but the blood on our hands gets thicker by the day. Accountability is long overdue. Let’s start here: spread the word and donate to Mike Gravel to get us on that damn stage. | | | | \- \- | | | | Paid for by the Committee for Peace, Justice, and Mike Gravel, a grassroots campaign committed to getting Senator Mike Gravel to the Democratic Debates. Contributions to the Committee for Peace, Justice, and Mike Gravel are not tax-deductible. | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Gravel 2020 15 Park Ave Ardsley, NY 10502-1621 * USA

#
I’ll be direct here: I’m emailing you to ask you to make your first contribution to our campaign for president. **[You can make a contribution with this link here »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190429-eom?refcode=ema190429-eom25-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=5&t=1&refcode2=4611_5755789_HTXNkR&akid=4611%2E5755789%2EHTXNkR)** Your contribution matters a lot right now. From the first day of this campaign, I made a commitment to you that I would reject any contributions from corporate PACs or federal lobbyists. Instead, this campaign is powered by people. It’s a deliberate choice. Even though it means leaving money on the table. Here is an uncomfortable fact about this race: _The president has already raised at least $30 million in his re-election bid._ He will raise -- and spend -- even more to hold on to power. We have to decide who we are, and whether we’re willing to fight for it. This will not be easy. subject: Our meeting with DCCC Chair Cheri Bustos

body:
I helped deliver over 30,000 petitions - signed by Our Revolution supporters like you
# [](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a506/bed43eb/9732d908/75fb6e92/452485529/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-header-OR")
**DCCC Blacklist: Campaign Update!**
Last week, I helped deliver over 30,000 petitions - signed by Our Revolution supporters like you across the USA - directly to Rep. Cheri Bustos, Chair of the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee! Because YOU took action, Rep. Bustos agreed to meet with us and hear why we are demanding she end the DCCC Blacklist immediately!
[**Our Revolution is on the frontlines of the fight to transform the Democratic Party. In just 3 weeks, we 've mobilized tens of thousands of people AND took our demand straight to the top of the DCCC. Will you make a contribution so that we can ramp up our campaign to END the DCCC BLACKLIST once and for all?**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a506/bed43eb/9732d908/75fb6e9d/452485529/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dccc-blacklist-petition?refcode=em190429-full&noskip=true")
[](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a506/bed43eb/9732d908/75fb6e9d/452485529/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dccc-blacklist-petition?refcode=em190429-full&noskip=true")
_Clem Balanoff from Our Revolution Illinois and Richard Rodriguez, National Our Revolution Board Member deliver petitions to Rep. Cheri Bustos._
Here's the update: Last week, I joined with other Our Revolution leaders for a frank sit-down meeting with Rep. Cheri Bustos in Illinois to deliver our message: Over 30,000 progressive activists who are members of Our Revolution think the DCCC Blacklist is bad politics and bad policy.
We told Rep. Bustos that the DCCC Blacklist is designed to protect incumbents and prevent a new generation of progressives - like Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Ro Khanna and Ayanna Pressley - from getting elected to Congress.
We told her that Our Revolution activists are laying the groundwork to elect more progressives across the country and we won't let the DCCC Blacklist undermine our critical work.
Rep. Bustos took our petitions, listened to our concerns, and agreed to set up a follow-up meeting with DCCC officials in Washington, DC. It's a step in the right direction - but we also made it clear that we won't stop organizing until she rescinds the DCCC Blacklist once and for all!
**[Because of our power to mobilize the grassroots, top DCCC officials are paying attention to our demands. But we still have more organizing work to do to get them to END the DCCC BLACKLIST. Will you make a $5 donation to Our Revolution and help us ESCALATE our campaign before our next meeting with DCCC officials?](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a506/bed43eb/9732d908/75fb6e9d/452485529/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dccc-blacklist-petition?refcode=em190429-full&noskip=true")**
Thank you for stepping up. When we fight together, we will win!
In Solidarity,
Richard Rodriguez
National Board Member
Our Revolution

name: Whitney Holt
subject: FW: Doing what we said
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
I hope you had a great weekend. Just checking in quickly to make sure you saw Abigail's email yesterday (see below) regarding our April fundraising deadline.
| | Hi Peter,

I hope you had a great weekend. Just checking in quickly to make sure you saw Abigail's email yesterday (see below) regarding our April fundraising deadline.

We're getting close to the 24 hour countdown, and we're working to ensure we are on track to hit our monthly goal.

As Abigail mentioned, beating our goals is what gave us the resources and firepower to defy the odds and win last November. We need the same edge as we head towards 2020 because we will need to work just as hard to keep this seat as we did to win it!

Peter, we still have $11,198 left to raise in order to reach tomorrow's fundraising goal. Please, can you help us meet this goal?

Here's the link to make a donation of whatever size works for you: [**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/spanbergerq22019**](https://act.myngp.com/el/WB53VJ2l5qT0JZ6Kcym1Y1AcH85ZEIbX7BJWDDq41DI=/1ZUHOUKS0cmKk3upAOyj0cffQrjgqeefmkDqutvQrI4=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.29_b1_wh_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)

I want to report strong fundraising numbers to Abigail! Can I count on you?

Thanks,
Whitney

\-----forwarded message-----
From: **Abigail Spanberger**
Date: Sun, Apr 28, 2019 at 8:03 PM
Subject: Doing what we said

Peter,

I'm writing today to ask if you would be willing to make a contribution to my re-election campaign.

But before I ask for your help raising the last [**$16,781 we need to hit our April fundraising goal,**](https://act.myngp.com/el/WB53VJ2l5qT0JZ6Kcym1Y1AcH85ZEIbX7BJWDDq41DI=/1ZUHOUKS0cmKk3upAOyj0cffQrjgqeefmkDqutvQrI4=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.29_b1_wh_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800) I'd like to fill you in on just how far we've come.

We don't have to look too far back to remember how it felt to have a representative who was afraid to meet constituents and refused to be accountable to voters.

And it wasn't that long ago that our district's representative was more committed to dismantling our healthcare system and blocking common sense gun violence prevention measures than he was to creating a more equitable economy.

A representative who was more passionate about rejecting the science of climate change than taking responsible steps toward protecting our shared environment.

As you already know, we've come a long way in a short time.

Since taking office, my team and I have worked to restore integrity to this role and redirect the privileges of this office away from rigid ideology and toward building a more cooperative, more compassionate approach to public service. I've been working hard to represent the people of our district, and that means, first and foremost, writing, championing and passing legislation that addresses the problems facing our country and our citizens. With my freshmen colleagues in Congress, we're proving people can, should and must expect more from their representatives.

But you also know that we didn't build a grassroots effort that won what the pundits called "unwinnable" overnight. It took months of building budgets, connecting with constituents, making calls, knocking doors, holding events and hitting **daily** , **weekly** , and **monthly** **fundraising goals** to cross the finish line victorious. In two days, we close the books on another one of these fundraising goals, but Peter, we still have to make progress towards our April 30th goal. [**To be exact, we 're working to raise $16,781 by the end of the month -- which is why I'm coming to you now. Will you consider making a donation so we can reach our April target and pass another milestone together?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/WB53VJ2l5qT0JZ6Kcym1Y1AcH85ZEIbX7BJWDDq41DI=/we7WzTC6oxFfiZIjbvpq2yxyW47Fhd2BumcAQM6zc5g=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.29_b1_wh_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800) | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://act.myngp.com/el/WB53VJ2l5qT0JZ6Kcym1Y1AcH85ZEIbX7BJWDDq41DI=/we7WzTC6oxFfiZIjbvpq2yxyW47Fhd2BumcAQM6zc5g=?refcode=em_fr_2019.04.29_b1_wh_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)
---
I'm incredibly grateful to have a grassroots team that steps up when it counts. With your help, I'm confident this will be another one of those times.

Thank you for everything,
_\-- Abigail_
| Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/WB53VJ2l5qT0JZ6Kcym1Y1AcH85ZEIbX7BJWDDq41DI=/BQ1OYTcmzMpg86Egthm6aiyEim8oElnShVRXCd7zqU8=)

If you'd only like to receive our most important email updates, you may [click here](https://act.myngp.com/el/WB53VJ2l5qT0JZ6Kcym1Y1AcH85ZEIbX7BJWDDq41DI=/JE1Snfk3qhyl6bGVSEIJlxZ1NxLy_hjJs00zOQih-gA=) to request fewer emails from Abigail's campaign.

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/WB53VJ2l5qT0JZ6Kcym1Y1AcH85ZEIbX7BJWDDq41DI=/Fwid2FXRlkUMjzuWcQYLKia7qyu0VRGuZS6_5bp81ic=).

Spanberger For Congress
PO Box 3121
Glen Allen, VA 23058

subject: Remembering Richard Lugar: American Statesman
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Indiana Republican Party
body:
Yesterday, Indiana lost a transformative leader, former U.S. Senator Richard Lugar. He was a mayor, a senator, a visionary, a leader, but most... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/remembering-richard-lugar-american-statesman?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
|
---
| Yesterday, Indiana lost a transformative leader, former U.S. Senator Richard Lugar. He was a mayor, a senator, a visionary, a leader, but most importantly, he was a Hoosier.

Hoosiers and Americans from across the country are coming together to mourn the loss of the ultimate statesman who leaves an immeasurable impact on the city of Indianapolis, the state of Indiana, our nation and our globe.
_**Here's what people are saying about the loss of Richard Lugar:**_
**Vice President Mike Pence:** _“Senator Lugar’s contributions to the life of our nation are countless. Richard Lugar was a leader not only in the Senate but also on the world stage, where he worked tirelessly to bring pressure to end apartheid in South Africa and enforce treaties that destroyed Soviet weapons of mass destruction. As the longest serving member of Congress from Indiana, and a Presidential Medal of Freedom recipient, he leaves behind a legacy of public service that will inspire Hoosiers for generations.”_

---
|
---
| **Governor Eric Holcomb:** _“The world weeps alongside Indiana after just learning we lost one of our best, ever. As an always faithful servant to the highest ideals in every walk of his incredible life, Richard Lugar ran the family farm, charted a new innovative course for Indiana’s capital city, and devoted a record six terms as a U.S. senator to making the world a more prosperous and peaceful place.

He was an officer and gentleman, father and faith leader, a mayor and senator, a diplomat and legendary role model to millions," Holcomb said. "Janet and I are keeping Mrs. Lugar and their wonderful family in our prayers and ask all those touched by his service to join us.”_

---
|
---
| **Director of National Intelligence Dan Coats:** _“Senator Richard Lugar was a true statesman whose decency and commitment to public service represented the very best of our state of Indiana and the nation. As both mayor (of Indianapolis) and senator, he demonstrated a unique ability to bring people together and forge coalitions to accomplish big things, particularly in foreign policy."_

---
|
---
| **Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch:** _"Sen. Lugar was an institution, a policymaker and true advocate for the state of Indiana. His work in Congress remains unparalleled. His political power influenced those around him, including myself. I first was inspired to enter politics in 1982, when I worked for him on his campaign trail. I know countless individuals who are in the leadership positions they are today, because of Sen. Lugar." _

---
|
---
| **Senator Todd Young:** _"As a boss, a mentor, and a person, Richard Lugar was the gold standard. At a time when nuclear proliferation was civilization’s greatest threat, Senator Lugar helped save the world. He leaves a legacy as an exemplar of wisdom, civility, and bipartisanship. Always staying true to his temperament, he was a quiet, dignified statesman. He thought before he spoke. He emphasized substance over personality. In short, he set the bar for public leaders — and for leaders more generally. I’m not sure we will ever see another Richard Lugar, but I pray that we do. May God watch over him and his family.”_

---
|
---
| **Senator Mike Braun:** _“Sen. Richard Lugar is a towering figure in Hoosier history and one of the greatest statesmen ever to serve in the U.S. Senate. As our longest serving Senator, he worked tirelessly with leaders across the globe to better the state and the country he loved. Maureen and I are praying for the Lugar family in this difficult time.”_

---
|
---
| **Former Governor and Purdue University President Mitch Daniels:** _“Words are hard to come by right now. Dick Lugar was not just the finest public servant I will ever know, he was the finest person. He embodied all we can hope for in our leaders: brilliance of mind, purity of motive, stainless in character, tireless in the pursuit of duty. Incomparably knowledgeable about the world, he was first and always a patriot, utterly dedicated to the security and wellbeing of his fellow Americans. His voice is now silent, but he is still with us. Indianapolis is a thriving and vibrant city because of him. The world is safer from nuclear danger because of him. And so many of us, while falling far short of the standards he set, are vastly better people because of him."_

---
|
---
| **Congresswoman Jackie Walorski:** _“Senator Richard Lugar was a Hoosier statesman without equal who worked tirelessly for peace, prosperity, and freedom at home and abroad. His wisdom, bipartisanship, and commitment to American ideals made our country stronger and the world safer. We mourn his loss, but we know his legacy will endure in those who follow his example of servant leadership.”_

---
|
---
| **Congressman Jim Banks:** _“Dick Lugar is a giant in the hearts and minds of Hoosiers and all who served with him during his career in public service and record-setting tenure in the Senate. His selfless service and tireless advocacy on behalf of the state and nation we both love inspired many to follow his footsteps into public service.”_

---
|
---
| **Congressman Jim Baird:** _"Senator Richard Lugar was a statesman to his very core, as he exemplified leadership and civility in his service to Indiana. I join Hoosiers and our nation as we mourn the passing of a truly remarkable public servant. Our prayers go out to the Lugar family and loved ones."_

---
|
---
| **Congresswoman Susan Brooks:** _“Today we lost a great man. Senator Richard Lugar dedicated his life to making the world a better and safer place.

“Senator Lugar was a veteran, Mayor of Indianapolis and the longest tenured Member of Congress to serve from Indiana who served Hoosiers with integrity and honor. He navigated foreign policy matters adeptly, leaving a safer world because of his work. He earned a Medal of Freedom for successfully convincing the former Soviet Union to dismantle their weapons at the end of the Cold War. Senator Lugar worked brilliantly not only on security issues, but also on those that impacted Americans every day, including food security and global hunger, education for young people, energy independence and free trade. But no matter the topic at hand, he always made time for the next generation, to teach and inspire young Hoosiers to serve their country, and to remind all of us that we have more in common as Americans than we do differences.

“It was an honor to be selected by him to serve as United States Attorney and we will miss his wisdom dearly, but his legacy will endure for generations to come. Please join me in sending our deepest condolences to the Lugar family during this time of reflection and sadness.”_

---
|
---
| **Congressman Greg Pence:** _"Sen. Lugar will be remembered as one of America's greatest Senators, leaving his mark on American and global affairs throughout his six terms. He will also be remembered here in Indiaan as a proud Hoosier who always kept our best interests in his heart. May God rest his soul. Our prayers are with Charlene and the entire family."_

---
|
---
| **Congressman Larry Bucshon:** _"Senator Richard Lugar was a giant in Indiana politics and a world leader on foreign policy. My condolences go out to his family and friends on his passing."_

---
|
---
| **Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer:** _"Today as Hoosiers and Americans, we mourn the loss of Richard Lugar, the ultimate statesman who leaves an immeasurable impact on the city of Indianapolis, the state of Indiana, our nation and our globe.

"Lugar was a Hoosier who dedicated his life to making the world a better a place, starting in his own hometown by ushering in a new era of modern government for Indianapolis. He brought new life to Indiana's capital city, with a visionary approach that catalyzed new growth and opportunity for the region.

"But Richard Lugar's impact is felt by all Americans and all people who are safe today because of his commitment to security and nuclear disarmament during the Cold War. As a U.S. Senator, Richard Lugar reduced the threat of nuclear weapons in the former Soviet Union through the Nunn-Lugar Act. His leadership in foreign relations was unparalleled.

"Serving Hoosiers for nearly four decades -- the longest serving U.S. Senator in Indiana's history -- when Richard Lugar spoke, you listened. His words carried immense weight and were always backed with knowledge and insight.

"Outside the Senate, Richard Lugar is also responsible for inspiring generations of Hoosier and American leaders who follow him. In particular, his leadership in helping get more women involved in public service through the Richard G. Lugar Excellence in Public Service Series is a legacy that will continue here in Indiana as more than 2,000 women have now completed the program.

"Through it all, Richard Lugar led with a humble spirit that represented our state and our people with honor. He was a farmer, a mayor, a senator, a visionary, a leader, but most importantly, a Hoosier. We express our deepest condolences to Char and his family, and we thank them for sharing Richard Lugar with the world."_

---
|
---
| **Indiana Commissioner for Higher Education Teresa Lubbers:** _“Like countless other people, I’m struggling with my emotions and sense of loss. There was no one like Dick Lugar, and it’s really hard to imagine the world without him. He never let us down — not the city, state, nation or world. The tributes will be pouring in and he deserves every one of them. I had the privilege to see the Senator and Char about a month ago at an exhibit for his papers — from school board to his current service at the Lugar Center. He was as gracious in manner and insightful in perspective as ever. I was able to tell him — again — that other than my faith and family, nothing has had more of an impact on my life than the inspiration he provided. The sadness I’m experiencing is tempered by the gratitude all of us should feel for his life and selfless service.”_

Hey Peter --

Sorry to bother you again, but we were going through our records and it looks like you haven't signed our petition to say you're on Team Joe.

**That means strengthening our middle class.**

This country wasn't built by bankers, CEOs or hedge fund managers, it was built by the American middle class. That's the story of America -- of ordinary people doing extraordinary things.

Across the country, too many families are being left out. There used to be a basic bargain in this country that when you work hard, you were able to share in the prosperity your work helped create. Joe wants to restore that basic bargain for American workers.

name: Cory Booker
subject: Can you hop on the phone tonight?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | Team -- Every day I'm reminded that I wouldn't be here without people like you. Your support has meant the world to me. That's why I want to take some time to fill you in on this campaign for president -- the reasons I'm running, and the latest updates from the trail. Can you hop on the phone with me tonight at 9 p.m. Eastern Time? _**[Sign up now to RSVP and send in any questions you want me to answer.](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc76db/5fca0d19/1cd7e58c/291ac7c3/433011758/VEsH/ "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/strategy-call?source=em_20190429_actives")**_ _**[https://act.corybooker.com/supporter-call](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc76db/5fca0d19/1cd7e58c/291ac7c3/433011758/VEsE/ "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/strategy-call?source=em_20190429_actives")**_ There are a record-setting number of Democrats running for president. I hope to earn voters' support in the weeks and months to come. And even though I'm so proud to have had you with me in the past, I want you to know I take nothing -- and no one -- for granted. Tonight, we'll talk about how we're building this campaign and what I'm hearing from voters as I travel the country. And we'll save some time for questions you have. _**[This is a conversation for folks who helped get me here, and I'd love it if you joined in. RSVP here for tonight's call.](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc76db/5fca0d19/1cd7e58c/291ac7c3/433011758/VEsF/ "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/strategy-call?source=em_20190429_actives")**_ Can't wait, Cory

subject: The deafening silence of the President’s critics
body:
It’s a playlist that seems stuck on repeat: Someone on the left makes a wild, baseless prediction that America’s economy will be destroyed... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=33a4c3ec22&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • April 29, 2019
---
| | |
# **The deafening silence of the President’s critics**

**It’s a playlist** that seems stuck on repeat: Someone on the left makes a wild, baseless prediction that America’s economy will be destroyed by the Presidency of Donald J. Trump . . . only to be proven wrong time and time again by every shred of evidence.

**It happened again** on Friday, as the U.S. economy beat expert expectations (sound familiar?) and grew at a powerful 3.2 percent. What’s more, the first quarter of a given year is often the slowest, meaning American GDP growth in 2019 could soar even higher.
**🎬****Watch:** [“It's a blowout number,” Larry Kudlow says](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d294870a06&e=3ee4985eea)
**When President Trump** took office in 2017 with a message of national renewal, he saw America as an engine of prosperity waiting to rev up again—a vision that most of Washington had written off years earlier. When he set the goal of returning to 3 percent growth, President Obama’s former economic adviser Larry Summers said that target was “fair enough if you believe in tooth fairies.”

**Of course** , that forecast pales in comparison to now-House Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s (D-CA) description of President Trump’s tax cuts as “Armageddon,” or to liberal economist Paul Krugman’s prediction of a “global recession, with no end in sight.”

**Yet the Trump Economy’s** strongest feature is even more underreported than its impressive top-line numbers. “Blue-collar workers are beating white-collar workers,” National Economic Council Director Larry Kudlow says. In other words, it’s the working class rather than the professional class that’s getting the biggest boost from [higher wages](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=087204d73f&e=3ee4985eea).

**None of this****happened** by accident. President Trump’s economic agenda from day one has been to rebuild the great American middle class from the bottom up. That means lower taxes, trade deals that put our workers first, American energy leadership, the return of manufacturing jobs, and a commitment to having the best infrastructure on Earth.

[By the numbers: 2019 starts off with a bang](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=64f2e8123b&e=3ee4985eea)

**Ben Carson** : [A new dawn for economic opportunity](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c2f49066d6&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## Video of the Day
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b14d680704&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **On Friday,** President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump welcomed Prime Minister Shinzo Abe and Mrs. Akie Abe of Japan to the White House. As part of the visit, the two First Ladies visited the Washington Monument and National Arboretum over the weekend.

**The U.S.–Japan alliance** has been a cornerstone for peace and prosperity in Asia—and around the world—for decades. Together, the economies of the United States and Japan account for about 30 percent of global GDP, meaning that strong ties between the two nations are essential for stability in the Indo-Pacific and beyond.

**In****May** , President Trump and the First Lady will travel to Japan to be honored as the first official State Guests following this week’s enthronement of Emperor Naruhito.
---
|
---
|
## **A message from President Trump on the Passover shooting**

**A****horrific shooting** took place at the Chabad of Poway Synagogue in California over this weekend as its congregation gathered for the last day of Passover. What was supposed to be a day of celebration ended in tragedy, leaving one dead and three injured.

**No community of faith** should ever be targeted for their beliefs, and no form of bigotry should be tolerated in the United States of America. Yesterday, President Trump spoke with Rabbi Yisroel Goldstein, who was injured during the weekend’s attack.

**In awful moments** like these, the very best of America often reveals itself. As the gunman opened fire, an off-duty U.S. Border Patrol agent stepped into action to prevent further loss of life. “Sincerest THANK YOU to our great Border Patrol Agent who stopped the shooter at the Synagogue in Poway, California,” the President shared on Twitter. “He may have been off duty but his talents for Law Enforcement weren’t!”

[Watch President Trump’s statement on the Passover shooting](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cde7a66228&e=3ee4985eea).

**More:** ‘[Off-duty Border Patrol agent who tried to stop synagogue shooting suspect hailed as hero,’ _Washington Examiner_ reports](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=389edfb621&e=3ee4985eea)
---
